page: Microsoft Azure E16s_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Esv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs derived from 8 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache. The system is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2096.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.4 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Multi-core performance is solid, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 15802.38 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 13015.0. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1232.73 GB/sec and a large block uncached write bandwidth of 68.96 GB/sec. Notably, the server achieves top-tier performance in LLM text generation for small (135M) models, reaching 366.52 tokens per second, while larger model inference and database operations fall into the average range.

Given its high memory density of 8 GB per vCPU and dedicated Intel Xeon compute resources, the E16s_v6 offers a balanced cost-to-resource profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The inclusion of advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) enhances its capability for vector and matrix operations. This configuration is highly suitable for memory-optimized workloads, including relational databases, in-memory caching systems, enterprise applications, and moderate-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E16s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv6 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs from 8 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores running at 4.2 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2096.0 and top-tier text generation speeds of 366.52 tokens per second for small LLM models. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain consistently in the average range. With advanced CPU flags like Intel AMX and AVX-512, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy operations. It is well-suited for database hosting, in-memory caching, enterprise applications, and text processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16s_v6 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance, making it ideal for database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high memory density for demanding database and enterprise workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong single-core performance and top-tier small LLM text generation speeds
Support for advanced instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512
Designed for memory-intensive workloads, databases, and enterprise applications
